title: System Inspection: Configuration & Procedures
description: Inspect system datasets, PROCLIB, and configuration. Map what a normal user can see.

steps.title: Navigate to ISPF Option 3.4
steps.screen:
ISPF Primary Option Menu
Option ===> 3

ISPF Utility Menu
Option ===> 4

DSLIST - Enter Dataset Pattern
Dsname Level ===> SYS1
steps.expected: Access the dataset list for SYS1 system datasets.
steps.hint: Type 3 at ISPF, then 4 for DSLIST, then enter SYS1 as the pattern.

steps.title: Inspect SYS1.PROCLIB
steps.screen:
BROWSE  SYS1.PROCLIB

Name     Prompt   Size  Created
COBOL             12   1999/01/01
FORTRAN           8    1999/01/01
IEFBR14           2    1999/01/01
JES2              45   1999/01/01
RACF              31   1999/01/01
STARTED           18   1999/01/01
TSO               22   1999/01/01

Command ===>
steps.expected: PROCLIB contains JCL procedures for started tasks and batch jobs.
steps.hint: Write access to PROCLIB = ability to modify how started tasks execute.

steps.title: View JES2 Proc
steps.expected: JES2 procedure defines how the job entry subsystem starts.
steps.hint: Modifying this proc or its PARMLIB member affects ALL job processing on the system.

steps.title: Check LINKLIST
steps.screen:
TSO COMMAND: D LINKLIST

CSF031I  14.22.35  LINKLIST DISPLAY  001
LINKLIST SET: LNKLST00
  SYS1.LINKLIB
  SYS1.MIGLIB
  SYS1.CSSLIB
  CEE.SCEERUN
  CEE.SCEERUN2

Any program in these libraries runs without APF authorization check.
steps.expected: LINKLIST libraries are searched for programs without explicit DSN specification.
steps.hint: Injecting a program into a LINKLIST library = arbitrary code execution for any user.

steps.title: APF List
steps.screen:
TSO COMMAND: D PROG,APF

CSF028I  14.22.40  APF LIST  001
FORMAT=DYNAMIC
VOLUME=MVSRES  DSNAME=SYS1.LINKLIB
VOLUME=MVSRES  DSNAME=SYS1.SVCLIB
VOLUME=MVSRES  DSNAME=SYS1.LPALIB

APF-authorized libraries can issue privileged SVCs.
steps.expected: APF-authorized libraries can run privileged code.
steps.hint: Write access to any APF library = privilege escalation to supervisor state.

steps.title: Inspection Summary
steps.screen:
System Inspection Complete

High-value targets identified:
- SYS1.PARMLIB  (system config)
- SYS1.PROCLIB  (started task JCL)
- SYS1.LINKLIB  (APF + LINKLIST)
- SYS1.LPALIB   (link pack area)

Any write access to these = system compromise.
Map RACF profiles for each before proceeding.
steps.expected: Identify the highest-value datasets for privilege escalation.
steps.hint: These four datasets are the crown jewels of z/OS security.

steps.title: Lab Complete
steps.screen:
Lab: System Inspection Complete

Key takeaways:
1. PROCLIB controls how started tasks run
2. LINKLIB/LPALIB = APF-authorized code paths
3. PARMLIB = system behavior at IPL
4. Normal users can READ most of these
5. WRITE access = full system control

Next: RACF Authorization Plane Analysis
steps.expected: Understand the attack surface of z/OS system datasets.
steps.hint: The z/OS attack surface is defined by who can write to these four dataset groups.
